title: NOC 21330 – Mining Engineers | GTR Immigration
description: Mining Engineers are in strong demand across Canada, driven by expansion in mineral exploration, clean-energy materials, infrastructure metals, and advanced mining technologies. Classified under NOC 21330 (TEER 1), this occupation fits well within:
duties: Conduct surveys and economic-environmental feasibility studies
Select safe and efficient mining methods
Design shafts, haulage routes, and ventilation systems
Build digital mine models and mapping systems
Supervise engineers, technicians, and survey teams
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in mining engineering or related discipline
Master’s/PhD for research or senior roles (sometimes)
job_outlook: This makes Mining Engineering moderately competitive, but highly attractive for candidates with:
pathways: Career growth in battery-minerals & green-energy mining
additional_information: Employers across Canada recruit Mining Engineers for:

Mineral exploration & feasibility studiesUnderground and open-pit mine planningVentilation and safety systemsDrill-and-blast optimizationAutomation & digital mine modelingSustainable and environmentally responsible operations
Annual salaries commonly range from $49,100–$155,500 CAD, depending on province, project type, and licensing level.
